title: Cactus elatior
intro: Cactus elatior, also known as the "devil's head cactus," is a species of flowering plant in the Cactaceae family, characterized by its distinctive, rounded stem and vibrant red flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cactus elatior include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Cactus elatior is often used to make herbal preparations such as tinctures, teas, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Cactus elatior is classified in the family Euphorbiaceae, but it is often mistakenly placed in the Cactaceae family due to its name. The correct classification reflects its botanical and morphological characteristics. It is native to South Africa and belongs to the genus Raphionacme. The species is further divided into two subspecies. Cactus elatior is an example of the misclassification of plants due to their appearance and name.
